stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Lemur catta is a primate found on the island of Madagascar.
  • Indri indri is a primate also found on Madagascar.
  • Conservation organizations evaluate the risk that species will become extinct in the near future.
  • L. catta faces an elevated risk of extinction.
  • I. indri faces a critically high risk of becoming extinct in the near future.
stem: The student wants to compare the extinction risk faced by L. catta with that faced by I. indri. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Conservation organizations have evaluated both L. catta's and I. indri's risk of becoming extinct in the near future.
L. catta is a primate that faces an elevated risk of extinction.
According to conservation organizations, I. indri faces a higher risk of becoming extinct in the near future than L. catta.
L. catta faces a critically high risk of extinction, while I. indri faces only an elevated risk of becoming extinct in the near future.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The sentence compares the extinction risks of the two species, noting that I. indri has a higher risk of becoming extinct in the near future than L. catta.

Choice A is incorrect. While the sentence mentions that conservation organizations have evaluated both species for extinction risk, it doesn't compare the extinction risks of the two species. Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence states that L. catta faces an elevated risk of extinction, it doesn't mention I. indri or compare the extinction risks of the two species. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence does compare the extinction risks of the two species, it misrepresents the notes; I. indri faces a higher risk than L. catta, not the other way around.
